pattern: Whether a query works comes down to comparing small per-letter tallies -- what each half's shuffle-able piece can supply versus what the untouched positions demand -- rather than ever building an actual rearrangement.
hints: Picture each half of the string split into the piece you are allowed to shuffle and the piece you are not; a position whose mirrored partner sits in the untouched piece still needs one specific fixed letter to land there, so track what the shuffle-able piece must supply against what it actually holds.

Any mirrored pair that is untouched on both sides can never change, so if even one such pair is already mismatched the whole query fails immediately -- check that first, using a running count of mismatches so it costs nothing extra per query.

For everything else, split each side's shuffle-able letters into 'must supply this exact fixed letter to the other side's untouched partner' (needs at least that many of each letter available, not just any count) and 'left over, paired freely against the other side's left-over letters' (needs the two left-over tallies to match exactly, letter for letter) -- one running count per letter over the whole string makes every one of these checks O(1).

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two independent queries on the same string
statement example, a query that cannot fix an untouched mismatched pair
statement example, overlapping freedom that still works out
smallest possible string, both halves are single untouched-equivalent positions that already mismatch
smallest possible string, both halves are single positions that already match
both halves fully free but their letter tallies can never be made to agree
both halves fully free and their letter tallies already agree
partial overlap where the shuffle-able piece is asked to supply a letter it does not contain at all

stress.description: a large string over a 3-letter alphabet with several thousand random valid queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    if n % 2 == 1:
        n -= 1
    if n < 2:
        n = 2
    alphabet = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    half = n // 2
    num_q = max(1, min(n, 3000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(num_q):
        a = rng.randint(0, half - 1)
        b = rng.randint(a, half - 1)
        c = rng.randint(half, n - 1)
        d = rng.randint(c, n - 1)
        queries.append([a, b, c, d])
    return [s, queries]

stress.description: a moderate-size string over a 2-letter alphabet with a very large number of queries, stressing the per-query check itself
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = 2000
    alphabet = 'ab'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    half = n // 2
    num_q = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(num_q):
        a = rng.randint(0, half - 1)
        b = rng.randint(a, half - 1)
        c = rng.randint(half, n - 1)
        d = rng.randint(c, n - 1)
        queries.append([a, b, c, d])
    return [s, queries]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def canMakePalindromeQueries(self, s: str, queries: List[List[int]]) -> List[bool]:
        n = len(s)
        half = n // 2
        pref = []
        for c in range(26):
            ch = chr(97 + c)
            arr = [0] * (n + 1)
            run = 0
            for i in range(n):
                if s[i] == ch:
                    run += 1
                arr[i + 1] = run
            pref.append(arr)
        mism_pref = [0] * (half + 1)
        for i in range(half):
            mism_pref[i + 1] = mism_pref[i] + (1 if s[i] != s[n - 1 - i] else 0)
        total_mismatch = mism_pref[half]

        def cnt(arr, l, r):
            if l > r:
                return 0
            return arr[r + 1] - arr[l]

        res = []
        for a, b, c, d in queries:
            lo = max(a, n - 1 - d)
            hi = min(b, n - 1 - c)
            inter_empty = lo > hi

            mismatch_lr = cnt(mism_pref, a, b)
            mismatch_rr = cnt(mism_pref, n - 1 - d, n - 1 - c)
            mismatch_inter = 0 if inter_empty else cnt(mism_pref, lo, hi)
            mismatch_outside = total_mismatch - (mismatch_lr + mismatch_rr - mismatch_inter)
            if mismatch_outside != 0:
                res.append(False)
                continue

            ok = True
            for ch_idx in range(26):
                arr = pref[ch_idx]
                cl = cnt(arr, a, b)
                cr = cnt(arr, c, d)
                f_full = cnt(arr, n - 1 - b, n - 1 - a)
                f_inter = 0 if inter_empty else cnt(arr, n - 1 - hi, n - 1 - lo)
                f_target = f_full - f_inter
                g_full = cnt(arr, n - 1 - d, n - 1 - c)
                g_inter = 0 if inter_empty else cnt(arr, lo, hi)
                g_target = g_full - g_inter
                if f_target > cl or g_target > cr or (cl - f_target) != (cr - g_target):
                    ok = False
                    break
            res.append(ok)
        return res

complexity.time: O(26 * (n + q)) for n = s.length and q = queries.length
